settings.title: Settings
settings.terminal.font_family_hint: Comma-separated font stack; leave empty for the default monospace.
settings.terminal.confirm_close: Confirm before closing a task with terminals
settings.terminal.confirm_close_hint: When off, closing a task skips the dialog and closes immediately, keeping the worktree.
settings.terminal.shell_integration_hint: Ephemeral injection at spawn so zsh reliably emits OSC 133 marks, sharply improving sniffing of command completion / input waits / exit codes. Never writes your .zshrc (temporary ZDOTDIR, gone when the terminal exits). Takes effect on newly opened terminals.
notify.global.title: Desktop Notifications
notify.global.hint: Fire system notifications on AI agent state changes. Plain shell commands stay silent.
notify.events.title: Events & Sounds
notify.event.waiting_input.hint: When an AI agent prompts for input
notify.event.done.hint: Notify when Claude/Codex finishes a turn
notify.quiet_hours.title: Quiet Hours
notify.quiet_hours.hint: No notifications during this window. start > end means overnight (e.g. 22:00 → 08:00).
notify.perm.default: Permission required to fire system notifications.
notify.perm.denied: Notifications are denied. Enable in System Settings → Notifications → VibeTerm.
settings.keys.title: Keybindings
settings.keys.hint: Click "Record" then press the new key; Esc to cancel. `Mod` = Cmd on macOS / Ctrl on Windows. Press a modifier twice (Cmd/Ctrl/Shift/Alt) to record as DoubleTap+... (avoids clash with agent TUI slash menus).
settings.keys.reset_all_confirm: Reset all keybindings to built-in defaults? Custom bindings will be discarded.

prompts.preset.explain.content:
Explain this code, focusing on the non-obvious parts:
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.fix-error.content:
This throws an error. Diagnose root cause and fix.

ERROR:
{{cursor}}

CODE:
prompts.preset.tests.content:
Write focused unit tests covering edge cases. Use the project's existing framework:
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.review.content:
Review this code for bugs, performance issues, and idiom violations. Be specific:
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.refactor.content:
Refactor for clarity and maintainability. Keep behavior identical, no scope creep:
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.commit-msg.content:
Write a concise conventional-commits message for the staged diff. Format `<type>: <subject>` with optional body. Type one of feat/fix/refactor/docs/test/chore/perf/ci. Diff:
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.create-pr.content:
Draft a pull request title (under 70 chars) and a body with ## Summary (3 bullets) and ## Test plan (checklist). Context:
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.plan.content:
Plan the implementation step by step BEFORE coding. List: files to touch, risks, validation strategy. Don't write code yet.
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.security.content:
Review for security issues: injection, secrets, unsafe input handling, auth/authz, OWASP Top 10. Be specific about each finding.
{{cursor}}
prompts.preset.docs.content:
Add concise doc comments explaining the WHY (not the WHAT). Skip obvious lines:
{{cursor}}
statusbar.title: Status Bar
statusbar.description: Each agent gets its own config — plain shell uses default, claude terminal uses claude, codex uses codex. Each profile previews with mock data, WYSIWYG.
statusbar.reset_confirm: Reset all profiles to default? Custom config will be lost.
statusbar.empty_preview: Preview shows here after adding widgets
statusbar.widget_desc.pr-status: Current branch PR status (requires gh CLI)
statusbar.widget_desc.codex-5h: 5-hour quota (auto-picked by window_minutes, aligned with Claude)
statusbar.widget_desc.codex-7d: Long-period quota (weekly/monthly window auto-picked by window_minutes; free is now monthly 30d)
statusbar.widget_desc.claude-tokens-toda: Cumulative tokens across all projects in last 24h
statusbar.widget_desc.flex-separator: Takes remaining width, pushes subsequent widgets right
statusbar.widget_desc.gauge-ctx: Current agent context % as semi-circle gauge
privacy.clipboard_images.title: Pasted image temp directory
settings.env.note: Note: applies to newly created terminals; running terminals are unaffected.
settings.env.empty: (empty) click + Add; typical keys: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / HTTP_PROXY
settings.env.section_proxy: [proxy] Proxy (auto-injects HTTP_PROXY / HTTPS_PROXY / NO_PROXY when enabled)
settings.cli.title: AI CLI detection
settings.privacy.desc: Pasted screenshots (clipboard bitmaps) are saved as PNG. Copied image files / file paths insert the original path directly and are not written here.
settings.privacy.clear_confirm: Clear all pasted images? This cannot be undone.
settings.privacy.no_migrate: (files in the old directory are not migrated automatically)
about.tagline:
A modern terminal manager built for vibe coding.

Local-first, CJK-native. Let the agents run — who's burning, who's gone out, who's calling you, all in plain sight. No intrusion, no login, no cloud.
about.principles.title: Principles
about.principle.zero_intrusion: Zero intrusion — agent state comes purely from sniffing and read-only watching; never writes ~/.claude·~/.codex, installs no hooks, runs no daemon. Not a single byte of your agent config is touched.
about.principle.multi_agent: Command many agents instead of drowning in them — not just running agents, but seeing a whole screen of them at a glance, by state and urgency.
about.principle.terminal: A terminal is a terminal — the core terminal experience, polished; never upstaging itself into a bloated agent workbench.
about.principle.cjk: Native CJK support — Unicode 15 graphemes, East-Asian wide chars, IME input, and copy that never splits surrogate pairs / ZWJ sequences.
about.principle.privacy: Local-first · privacy-first — no account, no telemetry, uploads nothing. Checks for updates once on launch by default (toggle off in Settings); otherwise it stays offline.
about.principle.oss: MIT open source — fully public, review and contributions welcome.
features.title: Core features
features.agent.sniff: Pure status sniffing — running / waiting-input / stalled / done, zero intrusion
features.agent.urgency: Stall detection + urgency ranking — the agent that needs you floats to the top
features.agent.monitor: Live usage monitoring — context %, 5h/7d quota, burn rate, cache TTL, cost
features.agent.stats: Usage stats panel — Claude/Codex tokens & cost estimates, offline, shareable
features.terminal.split: N-ary splits + git worktree mounting, one terminal tree per task
features.terminal.canvas: Canvas view — task cards, marquee select, broadcast a command to many terminals
features.terminal.floating: Floating windows — pop a task into its own window
features.terminal.render: GPU rendering (WebGL) + native CJK — smooth, never drops glyphs
features.productivity.palette: Command palette + customizable shortcuts + custom actions
features.productivity.prompts: Prompt template library — presets for claude / codex / shell
features.productivity.statusbar: Highly configurable status bar — drag to arrange widgets, per-agent profiles
features.productivity.notify: Desktop notifications — 24 built-in sounds + quiet hours, only on agent state changes
features.productivity.theme: Built-in themes with hot-reload · cross-platform macOS / Windows
about.special_thanks.title: Special thanks
about.special_thanks.ccusage: Usage aggregation, model pricing and the 5-hour block logic are ported from / inspired by ryoppippi's ccusage (MIT). Pricing data traces back to LiteLLM and Anthropic's official pricing.
about.inspiration.title: Inspiration & references
about.inspiration.desc: Several parts of VibeTerm learned from these excellent projects — with thanks:
about.inspire.litellm: model price table (data source for price updates)
about.credits.title: Credits
about.credits.foot: Font: JetBrains Mono; notification sounds from Pixabay; full dependencies and licenses in the repo's Cargo.toml / package.json and THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md.
update.app.title: App update
update.app.install_error: In-app install failed; use the download page instead
update.app.error: Check failed (check your network and retry)
update.app.rate_limited: Check failed (GitHub API rate limit — try again later; not a network issue)
update.app.note: Checks GitHub Releases on launch by default (toggle below); read-only version compare, no telemetry, never uploads. "Download & install" verifies the minisign signature, updates in place, and restarts; if it fails use "Open download page".
update.price.title: Model pricing (estimates)
update.price.purpose: Costs shown in the usage panel are estimated from public list prices — not your actual Anthropic bill, for reference only. After Anthropic changes prices, update the price table here so past and future estimates track the latest pricing, no app upgrade needed. List prices come from the public table maintained by LiteLLM (BerriAI/litellm; with tiered pricing, sourced from Anthropic's official pricing); fetched manually, stored locally only.
update.price.failed: Update failed (check your network and retry)
update.price.note: Manual trigger, fetches the price table only at this moment; data is stored in local config and can be reset to the built-in default anytime.
settings.language.hint: Switch the interface language. Changes apply instantly.
notify.multiagent.title: Multi-agent alerts
notify.focused_other.title: Alert when another task finishes
notify.focused_other.hint: While you're in VibeTerm looking at one task, play a soft chime and highlight the row when a different task finishes.
notify.dock_badge.title: Dock badge for unseen completions
notify.dock_badge.hint: Show the number of finished-but-unseen tasks on the Dock icon; clears when you open the task.
notify.persistent.title: Persistent sound until seen
notify.persistent.hint: While VibeTerm is in the background, chime at the interval below as long as a finished task is unseen, until you come back. One sound, never stacks.
diff.title: Diff
update.app.auto_check_hint: Only a read-only version compare against GitHub; uploads nothing, no telemetry. Turn off to stay fully offline.
update.app.running_warn: {count} terminal(s) running. Installing will restart and interrupt them (your task list is kept). Continue?
